resources.title: Maintenance Rights of Women
resources.overview: A wife who is unable to maintain herself can claim maintenance from her husband under the relevant provisions of Indian law.
resources.law: Section 125 CrPC / Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005

faqs.question: Can a working woman claim maintenance?
faqs.answer: Yes, depending upon her income and circumstances.

resources.title: Protection from Domestic Violence
resources.overview: The Domestic Violence Act protects women against physical, emotional, sexual, verbal and economic abuse.
resources.law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
resources.timeline: Emergency orders may be issued quickly.

faqs.question: Can I stay in my matrimonial home?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Act protects your right to residence.

resources.title: How to File an FIR
resources.overview: Women can file an FIR for cognizable offences at any police station.

faqs.question: Can police refuse to register FIR?

resources.title: Child Custody Rights
resources.overview: Parents can approach Family Court for custody of minor children.

faqs.question: Who gets child custody?
faqs.answer: The court decides based on the child's welfare.

resources.title: Property Rights of Women
resources.overview: Daughters have equal rights in ancestral property.

faqs.question: Can daughters inherit property?
faqs.answer: Yes. Daughters have equal coparcenary rights.

resources.title: Free Legal Aid
resources.overview: Women, children and economically weaker sections are entitled to free legal aid.

faqs.question: Who is eligible?
faqs.answer: Women are eligible irrespective of income.
